description: Accept an artifact's current content as the new drift baseline — a two-sided decision. `disposition` is required: `design_holds` (the change carries no design meaning; recorded as a dated claim) or `design_updated` (behaviour moved and the design moved with it; pass `design_change_event_id` from the record_change that updated it, so code and design are one change). Silent accept does not exist: it is how a design erodes into fiction over N fix cycles while reporting zero gaps. Until you accept, the same checksum_change is reported on every reconcile.

at.description:
Timestamp for the claim (reflow2 takes no clock). A dated claim is what
the confirmation ledger can report as "last checked at …".

checksum.description: The accepted content hash — the new drift baseline.

design_change_event_id.description:
For `design_updated`: the ChangeEvent recorded when the design was
updated. Must exist — a dangling reference is refused.

disposition.description:
The answer to the second question — required, because "accept the file,
leave the design alone, say nothing" is the option that erodes a design
(BL-33). `design_holds`: the change carries no design meaning (a
refactor, a fix restoring intended behaviour) — recorded as a dated
claim. `design_updated`: behaviour moved and the design moved with it —
pass `design_change_event_id` from the `record_change` that updated it.

note.description: Optional note stored on the recorded claim (`design_holds` only).

name: set_artifact_checksum
